title: A Short History of the World
description: Wells's compressed global history, written from a secular and internationalist viewpoint.
descriptionParagraphs: Wells's compressed global history, written from a secular and internationalist viewpoint.

A Short History of the World is organized less as a story than as an argument. As a history, it tries to persuade readers through selection, emphasis, and direct claims about history, internationalism, and secularism.

Its significance lies in the way it compresses large claims into memorable formulas and positions. Even readers who reject the work usually have to reckon with how sharply it frames questions about history, internationalism, and secularism.

overview.englishDescription: Wells's compressed global history, written from a secular and internationalist viewpoint.
banningParagraphs: A Short History of the World entered censorship debates as a history associated with history, internationalism, and secularism. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around ideological control and secularism.

The earliest event currently captured here is 20th century in Spain, where Francoist censors banned circulation. Broad, secular world histories clashed with dictatorship-era ideological constraints. This shows censorship reaching well beyond fiction into narrative explanations of the world.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
counterReadings.author: Hannah Arendt
counterReadings.note: A foundational analysis of state terror, propaganda, and ideological conformity.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: A short modern guide to resisting authoritarian politics and controlled public discourse.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
